early_learner.label: Early Learner (Ages 3-5)
early_learner.vocabulary: 500-1000 most common words; concrete nouns and simple verbs
early_learner.assumed_knowledge: everyday objects, family, animals, basic colours/counting
early_learner.audience_mindset: A pre-reader. This is a script an adult reads aloud while pointing at pictures. Wonder and repetition matter more than information density — one idea, felt rather than explained.

young_child.label: Young Child (Ages 6-10)
young_child.vocabulary: common words; define new words inline with concrete examples
young_child.audience_mindset: Just gaining reading independence. Anchor every new idea to something in their own daily world. Curiosity is the engine: make them want to know what comes next.

middle_schooler.label: Middle Schooler (Ages 11-13)
middle_schooler.vocabulary: domain terms okay if defined on first use
middle_schooler.assumed_knowledge: basic math, intro science, some critical thinking
middle_schooler.audience_mindset: Capable of real abstraction and highly alert to condescension. Respect their intelligence — the failure mode at this age is talking down, not over-complexity.

high_schooler.label: High Schooler (Ages 14-18)
high_schooler.vocabulary: domain terminology, defined on first use
high_schooler.audience_mindset: Near-adult reasoning. They can hold nuance and competing views. Engage them as thinkers, not as passive receivers of facts.

adult_beginner.label: Adult Beginner
adult_beginner.vocabulary: define all domain jargon; everyday adult language otherwise
adult_beginner.assumed_knowledge: general adult life experience only; nothing field-specific
adult_beginner.audience_mindset: A fully capable adult reasoner with ZERO knowledge of this topic. The gap is domain knowledge, not intelligence. Never condescend and never use childish analogies — but never assume prior exposure either. Anchor to common adult experience (work, money, driving, cooking).

adult_intermediate.label: Adult Intermediate
adult_intermediate.vocabulary: field terminology free; define only genuinely novel terms
adult_intermediate.tone: peer-to-peer, practical, includes trade-offs and comparisons
adult_intermediate.audience_mindset: Has the fundamentals and wants to deepen and connect. Do not re-teach basics. The value you add is nuance, trade-offs, and how the pieces fit together.

adult_advanced.label: Adult Advanced
adult_advanced.vocabulary: full terminology, no definitions of standard terms
adult_advanced.tone: precise, rigorous, peer; engages controversy and open problems
adult_advanced.audience_mindset: An expert or near-expert. Over-explanation is insulting; precision and density are the value. Engage with edge cases, tensions, and open questions.

overview.scope: 1-2 core ideas plus why it matters; no edge cases or mechanism detail

balanced.scope: core concepts plus key support plus 1-2 examples; minimal edge cases

comprehensive.scope: core plus nuance, edge cases, exceptions, connections, mechanism — bounded by the level concept cap

posture_explanation.leading: The image carries the meaning; the words support it. Favour placing both images.
posture_explanation.integrated: Place each image right next to the concept it shows; generous use helps.
posture_explanation.supportive: Place an image where it clarifies a specific concept; do not place it just because one exists.
posture_explanation.sparing: Only place an image for an idea that genuinely benefits from a visual; prefer fewer.
posture_explanation.diagram_only: Place only if the illustration is a true diagram or structure that conveys something prose can't. Otherwise omit.

caption_guidance.early_learner: one short, concrete spoken line a caregiver can read aloud
caption_guidance.young_child: one simple sentence naming what's shown
caption_guidance.middle_schooler: a short caption linking the image to the concept
caption_guidance.high_schooler: a precise caption stating what the image conveys
caption_guidance.adult_beginner: a clear caption that orients a newcomer
caption_guidance.adult_advanced: a precise technical label / figure caption
